operating_state: Prologis generated consolidated revenues of $4.8 billion in 2021 with rental revenues increasing to $4.1 billion driven by positive rental rate growth and the February 2020 Liberty Transaction acquisition. Core FFO attributable to common stockholders increased to $3.2 billion from $2.9 billion with Real Estate Operations NOI reaching $3.1 billion. Occupancy reached 97.7% at year-end with development completions contributing additional NOI growth through 62 projects stabilizing at 6.1% weighted average yield.
cost_structure: Rental expenses increased to $1.0 billion from $952 million while G&A expenses rose driven by higher compensation costs from outperformance. Interest expense decreased to $266 million from $315 million despite higher debt balances due to refinancing activities that reduced the weighted average effective rate from 2.2% to 1.7%. Development costs faced upward pressure from labor shortages and supply chain disruptions affecting material costs.
material_events: Completed $2.9 billion in senior note issuances with 1.0% weighted average rate and 14.2-year term while redeeming $1.5 billion of higher-cost debt, incurring $187 million in extinguishment losses. Generated $5.2 billion in net proceeds from property contributions to co-investment ventures and dispositions, realizing $1.6 billion in net gains. Development starts totaled 78 projects compared to 42 projects in 2020.
macro_exposures: Foreign currency exposure totaled $9.1 billion with 68% of debt denominated in non-USD currencies including Euro, Japanese Yen, and British Pound Sterling. Recognized $164 million in foreign currency and derivative gains compared to $167 million losses in 2020. COVID-19 pandemic effects continued creating uncertainty around customer payment capacity and development completion timelines.
forward_outlook: Development pipeline totals $4.7 billion with remaining spend across 99 properties that are 54.6% leased. Management expects continued increases in market rents and asset valuations to drive 2022 operating results with $5.0 billion in available liquidity. Co-investment venture equity commitments total $6.1 billion with debt maturities scheduled for 2022 repayment.
uncertainty_profile: REIT qualification requirements create ongoing compliance obligations that could limit operational flexibility while LIBOR to SOFR transition by June 2023 affects variable-rate debt pricing mechanisms. Co-investment ventures carry non-recourse third-party debt requiring refinancing coordination. Environmental remediation liabilities exist across the portfolio with potential for additional costs from regulatory changes.
